title: Aussie  Witchetty Grubs
content_raw:
```html

Aussie Witchetty Grubs

Aussie Witchetty Grubs are a yellow and white jelly based confectionery in the shape of a worm – with a smiling face. They are a similar consistency to the old fashion milk bottles and teeth. These taste similar to ice cream cones but are less cute and more insect-y.

Aussie Witchetty Grubs

You will get 30 worms in 100g and they contain glucose syrup, gelatine, dextrine, flavours, soy protein, salt, colours, water. They are suitable for gluten, dairy, and nut free dietary constraints/choices.

What Are Real Witchetty Grubs?

For those of you outside Australia, a witchetty grub (also spelled witchety grub or witjuti grub) is a term used here for the large, white, wood-eating larvae of several moths. It particularly applies to the larvae of the cossid moth (Endoxyla leucomochla), which feeds on the roots of the Witchetty bush (named after the grub) that is found in central Australia. The grub is the most important insect food in the desert. Edible either raw or lightly cooked in hot ashes, they are sought out as a high-protein food by indigenous Australians.

Lolly witchetty grubs don't need to be cooked (and I don't think they could be considered a source of protein) but they are still delicious and fun! If you're looking for fun novelty lollies like this, you might also enjoy exploring the best candy options for kids in Australia, which features all sorts of entertaining shapes and flavours. Why not try some Aussie Witchetty Grubs today?

  • Allseps AUSSIE WITCHETTY GRUBS 1kg$20

Perfect for Parties and Lolly Bags

These cheeky worm-shaped treats are perfect for adding a bit of fun to lolly bag ideas for kids, or if you're planning something bigger, you can find a full range of bulk lollies in Australia to suit any occasion. Whether you're stocking up for a party or just treating yourself, Aussie Witchetty Grubs are a uniquely Australian confectionery experience.

Frequently Asked Questions

What flavour do Aussie Witchetty Grubs taste like?

Aussie Witchetty Grubs taste similar to ice cream cones, offering a sweet and creamy flavour. They have a jelly-based texture comparable to classic Australian lollies like milk bottles and teeth.

Are Witchetty Grubs suitable for people with allergies?

Yes, Aussie Witchetty Grubs are suitable for gluten-free, dairy-free, and nut-free diets. However, they do contain gelatine and soy protein, so those with soy allergies should avoid them.

How many Witchetty Grubs come in a 100g bag?

A 100g bag contains 30 witchetty grubs, making them a generous serving of this fun worm-shaped confectionery.

What are real witchetty grubs and why are these lollies named after them?

Real witchetty grubs are large white larvae of moths found in central Australia, and they're an important high-protein food source for indigenous Australians. The candy lollies are named after these insects and shaped to resemble them, though they're purely a sweet treat rather than a nutritional food source.

What are the main ingredients in Aussie Witchetty Grubs?

Aussie Witchetty Grubs are made from glucose syrup, gelatine, dextrine, flavours, soy protein, salt, colours, and water. They're a jelly-based confectionery with a texture similar to traditional Australian lollies.

```
